title: Settings Page (app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx)
content:
This file defines the main settings page for the DocMed application. ## Purpose The Settings page allows users to manage their account profile and billing information. It utilizes a tabbed interface to switch between these two sections. ## Key Components and Functionality - **`SettingsPage` Component:** The main React component that renders the settings UI. - Manages the current active tab (`profile` or `billing`) using the `useState` hook. - Displays a main heading and description for the settings section. - Renders a tab bar with two buttons: 'Profile' and 'Billing'. - Conditionally renders content based on the active tab. - **`TabButton` Component:** A reusable button component for the tab bar. - Takes `active` (boolean), `onClick` (function), and `icon` (React component) as props. - Applies styling based on whether the tab is active or not. - **`BillingPlaceholder` Component:** A placeholder component for the billing section. - Displays a message indicating that payment management features are coming soon. - Includes an icon and descriptive text. - **`@clerk/nextjs` `UserProfile` Component:** Integrated for handling user profile management, including appearance customization for a consistent look and feel. ## Usage The `SettingsPage` component is intended to be used as a route within the application, likely at `/app/dashboard/settings`. ```tsx // Example of how SettingsPage might be imported and used in another component or layout import SettingsPage from './settings/page'; function DashboardLayout() { return (
{/* Other layout elements */} {/* Other layout elements */}
); } export default DashboardLayout; ``` ### Tab Switching Users can click on the 'Profile' or 'Billing' tabs to switch between the respective sections. The 'Profile' tab integrates with Clerk's `UserProfile` component for seamless profile updates. The 'Billing' tab currently displays a placeholder, indicating future functionality.
